what_it_does: Defines a Vi diamond avatar component that renders an SVG diamond with a gold-line hatch pattern, optional opacity animation, and an overlaid "Vi" glyph, then exposes it as window.ViMark.
observations: No imports are present; the file assumes a global JSX/React runtime.
A random id is generated on every render for the SVG pattern definition.
The diamond is drawn with two overlapping polygons: one white fill with a gold stroke, and one filled with the hatch pattern.
The hatch pattern uses #E0C010 horizontal lines and an SMIL <animate> when the animated prop is true.
An optional glyph renders a "V" with a smaller "i" nested in a span.
The component is assigned to window.ViMark, suggesting a non-module/global usage context.
standouts: Brand avatar component with hardcoded gold (#E0C010) diamond geometry.
Randomized SVG pattern id per render.
SMIL-based opacity animation gated by the animated prop.
Global window assignment rather than ES module export.
conventions: Functional component with destructured props and default values.
Inline styles mixed with SVG attributes.
Hardcoded brand color values instead of theme tokens.
Global registration via window for non-module environments.
concerns: Math.random in render can produce unstable ids and hydration mismatches if server-rendered.
Pattern id collisions are possible, though unlikely with a 5-character suffix.
window.ViMark global assignment may clash with other scripts.
No accessibility attributes on the SVG or glyph spans.
Color values are hardcoded rather than referencing a theme.
notes: A brand avatar component that renders a gold-hatched diamond with optional animation and a "Vi" glyph, exposed globally for non-module consumption.
questions: What runtime environment expects window.ViMark instead of an ES module export?
Is the size prop interpreted in pixels, and are units handled consistently?
What font family is intended for the glyph, since none is specified here?
Is the animated prop intended for all contexts or only specific interactive states?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of being the Vi diamond avatar matches the implementation.
intention_signals: Provide a reusable, recognizable Vi logo mark with a textured diamond shape, subtle animation, and a typographic glyph overlay.
novelty: Combines an SVG polygon hatch pattern with SMIL animation and an overlaid glyph inside a single avatar component, then registers it globally.
contribution: A concrete avatar implementation that captures the Vi brand geometry, gold hatch texture, optional animation, and glyph overlay as a visual identity primitive.
summary_for_embedding: ViMark.jsx defines a Vi diamond avatar component using an SVG polygon with a gold-line hatch pattern, optional SMIL opacity animation, and an overlaid "Vi" glyph, exposed as window.ViMark.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a reusable brand avatar/logo mark for the ConceptV design system with optional motion and glyph.

symbols.code_id: code://counterpart-design/reference/ConceptV Design System-handoff/conceptv-design-system/project/ui_kits/vi/ViMark.jsx::ViMark | component | function ViMark({ size = 32, animated = false, showGlyph = true }) {
symbols.does: Renders the Vi diamond avatar with optional animation and glyph.
symbols.description: ViMark is a functional component that builds a square container sized by the size prop, draws a rotated-square (diamond) SVG polygon with a white fill, gold stroke, and gold-line hatch pattern, optionally animates the pattern line opacity via SMIL, and overlays a "Vi" glyph when showGlyph is true. It is assigned to window.ViMark at the end of the file.
